quote.msg: 其實唔難，操熟reading listening，其他就有得拉

replies.msg: Agreed 100%. My approach to IELTS reading & listening prep is to really read a lot and listen a lot. It's not that difficult to do nowadays with Netflix and YouTube. You may use English subtitles to help. Read about what you like in English. It will never be boring since it's what you like. After all these, grab an IELTS book to familiarise yourself with the exam paper. Also take note of the differences between IELTS and DSE. You'll be good to go.

quote.msg: 其實唔難，操熟reading listening，其他就有得拉

Agreed 100%. My approach to IELTS reading & listening prep is to really read a lot and listen a lot. It's not that difficult to do nowadays with Netflix and YouTube. You may use English subtitles to help. Read about what you like in English. It will never be boring since it's what you like. After all these, grab an IELTS book to familiarise yourself with the exam paper. Also take note of the differences between IELTS and DSE. You'll be good to go.

replies.msg: Exactly. With such preparation anyone can also nail writing too. Just bear in mind that never ever write long sentences and you’ll be all set. For speaking, it’s really a matter of luck since the topic may not be something you have a whole lot of knowledge. I was asked to talk about my favourite artist in my first attempt and I totally had no idea.

quote.msg: 其實唔難，操熟reading listening，其他就有得拉

Agreed 100%. My approach to IELTS reading & listening prep is to really read a lot and listen a lot. It's not that difficult to do nowadays with Netflix and YouTube. You may use English subtitles to help. Read about what you like in English. It will never be boring since it's what you like. After all these, grab an IELTS book to familiarise yourself with the exam paper. Also take note of the differences between IELTS and DSE. You'll be good to go.

Exactly. With such preparation anyone can also nail writing too. Just bear in mind that never ever write long sentences and you’ll be all set. For speaking, it’s really a matter of luck since the topic may not be something you have a whole lot of knowledge. I was asked to talk about my favourite artist in my first attempt and I totally had no idea.

replies.msg: No time to polish your thoughts during speaking test I've also heard from some YouTubers saying that British people prefer to say a lot to keep the conversation going and to sound more polite. If that's the case, it's also advisable to practise this aspect.

Angel B: Emperor?

Angel B: Have you heard of Emperor?

Henchmen: Which one?

Angel B: What Emperor? No one seems to know you!

Angel B: I am Angel B, do you know me?

Angel B: No one seems to know you.

Angel B: Want to act like you are something, right?

Emperor: I am Handsome!

Angel B: Handsome? Where? You handsome?

Angel B: Damn you!

Emperor: Sorry everyone.

Angel B: Go to hell!

ielts 7.5

There is no any shortcut to learn other language . Just try your very best to create an environment using English .

few tips may work for you .

Not only memorise thousands of word and figure out the parts of speech of what you read daily but also talk to someone and listen from the native speakers through movies opening the subtitles in English and Chinese

The importance of former and the letters are equal .

And the most important, don’t be embarrassed when you make mistakes ,Miles begin with single step and omit naysayers around you .
